title: Create Metric Definitions in Tableau Pulse from DataTools Pro
description:
Integrate with Tableau Pulse REST API to create metric definitions from DataTools Pro converted metrics.

### Tableau Pulse Metric Definition Payload
Requires: `name`, `description`, `specification` (datasource ID, measure field + aggregation, time dimension), filters, extension options (dimensions, granularities), representation options (format type, sentiment), and comparison configuration.

### Field Mapping
- **Name**: metric name from DataTools Pro
- **Description**: metric description
- **Datasource ID**: obtained from the sheet's source when fetching dashboard fields
- **Measure**: field categorized as a measure from the datasource
- **Time Dimension**: field categorized as a dimension with time characteristics
- **Filters/Dimensions**: deferred for initial implementation

### Current Challenges
- Developer getting 404 errors when submitting metric definitions with field values derived from GraphQL data
- Possible mismatch between GraphQL field identifiers and REST API expected field identifiers
- Conflict (409) errors when using values from existing metric definitions
acceptance_criteria: Metric definition can be created in Tableau Pulse from a DataTools Pro converted metric
Datasource ID, measure, and time dimension are correctly populated
API errors are handled gracefully with user feedback
middleware_changes: Integration with Tableau Pulse REST API for metric definition creation. Field mapping from DataTools Pro metric data to Tableau Pulse payload format.
api_changes: Tableau Pulse REST API calls for creating metric definitions with datasource, measure, time dimension, and comparison configuration.
implementation_notes: Tableau Pulse API requires specific field identifiers that may differ from GraphQL field IDs. Initial scope covers name, description, datasource, measure, and time dimension. Filters and dimensions deferred. Postman workspace reference: https://www.postman.com/my-api-lab/tableau-pull-request-workspace/
comment_summary: Developer mapped DataTools Pro fields to Tableau Pulse payload and encountered 404 and 409 errors. The mismatch between GraphQL field identifiers and REST API identifiers is the primary blocker. Initial implementation focuses on name, description, datasource, measure, and time dimension only.
